Office visits and full or partial screenings conducted on the same day by the same care provider are not covered unless medical necessity is noted in the member’s record [1]. llm_answer: Services for EPSDT under Florida Medicaid should be billed using Current Procedural Terminology (CPT®) codes, Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) codes, and/or revenue codes [2]. These codes denote the services and/or procedures performed and must be fully supported in the medical record and/or office notes [2]. These reimbursement policies serve as a guide for accurate claims submissions [2].

llm_answer: Florida Medicaid EPSDT services for recipients under 21 years of age are billed using appropriate CPT or HCPCS procedure codes. All services provided to Medicaid recipients under 21 must be billed as EPSDT services, unless specifically excluded by federal or state law.
